author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter VII. 37–39.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Among the dissensions and doubtings of the Jews concerning the Lord Jesus Christ, among other things which He said, by which some were confounded, others taught: “On the last day of that feast” (for it was then that these things were done) which is called the feast of tabernacles; that is, the building of tents, of which feast you remember, my beloved, that we have already discoursed, the Lord Jesus Christ calls, not by speaking in any way soever, but by crying aloud, that whoso thirsts may come to Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If we thirst, let us come; and not by our feet, but by our affections; let us come, not by removing from our place, but by loving.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Although, according to the inner man, he that loves does also move from a place.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But it is one thing to move with the body, another thing to move with the heart: he migrates with the body who changes his place by a motion of the body; he migrates with the heart who changes his affection by a motion of the heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou lovest one thing, and didst love another thing before, thou art not now where thou wast.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, the Lord cries aloud to us: for, “He stood and cried out, if any man thirst, let him come unto me, and drink.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that believeth on me, as the Scripture saith, out of his belly shall flow rivers of living water.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We are not obliged to delay to inquire what this meant, since the evangelist has explained it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For why the Lord said, “If any man thirst, let him come unto me, and drink;” and, “He that believeth on me, out of his belly shall flow rivers of living water;” the evangelist has subsequently explained, saying: “But this spake He of the Spirit which they that believe on Him should receive.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Spirit was not yet given, because Jesus was not yet glorified.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is therefore an inner thirst and an inner belly, because there is an inner man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And that inner man is indeed invisible, but the outer man is visible; but yet better is the inner than the outer.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And this which is not seen is the more loved; for it is certain that the inner man is loved more than the outer.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How is this certain?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let every man prove it in himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For although they who live ill may surrender their minds to the body, yet they do wish to live, and to live is the property of the mind only; and they who rule, manifest themselves more than those things that are ruled.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now it is minds that rule, bodies are ruled.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Every man rejoices in pleasure, and receives pleasure by the body: but separate the mind from it, and nothing remains in the body to rejoice; and if there is joy of the body, it is the mind that rejoices.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If it has joy of its dwelling, ought it not to have joy of itself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if the mind has whereof it may have delight outside itself, does it remain without delights within?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is quite certain that a man loves his soul more than his body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But further, a man loves the soul even in another man more than the body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is it that is loved in a friend, where the love is the purer and more sincere?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What in the friend is loved—the mind, or the body?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If fidelity is loved, the mind is loved; if benevolence is loved, the mind is the seat of benevolence: if this is what thou lovest in another, that he too loves thee, it is the mind thou lovest, because it is not the flesh, but the mind that loves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For therefore thou lovest, because he loves thee: ask why he loves thee, and then see what it is thou lovest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consequently, it is more loved, and yet is not seen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I would say something further, by which it may more clearly appear to you, beloved, how much the mind is loved, and how it is preferred to the body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Those wanton lovers even, who delight in beauty of bodies, and are charmed by shapeliness of limbs, love the more when they are loved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For when a man loves, and finds that he is regarded with hatred, he feels more anger than liking.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why does he feel anger rather than liking?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because the love that he bestows is not given him in return.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, therefore, even the lovers of bodies desire to be loved in return, and this delights them more when they are loved, what shall we say of the lovers of minds?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if the lovers of minds are great, what shall we say of the lovers of God who makes minds beautiful?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as the mind gives grace to the body, so it is God that gives grace to the mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is only the mind that causes that in the body by which it is loved; when the mind has left it, it is a corpse at which thou hast a horror; and how much soever thou mayest have loved its beautiful limbs, thou makest haste to bury it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence, the ornament of the body is the mind; the ornament of the mind is God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord, therefore, cries aloud to us to come and drink, if we thirst within; and He says that when we have drunk, rivers of living water shall flow from our belly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The belly of the inner man is the conscience of the heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Having drunk that water then, the conscience being purged begins to live; and drinking in, it will have a fountain, will be itself a fountain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is the fountain, and what the river that flows from the belly of the inner man?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Benevolence, whereby a man will consult the interest of his neighbor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if he imagines that what he drinks ought to be only for his own satisfying, there is no flowing of living water from his belly; but if he is quick to consult for the good of his neighbor, then he becomes not dry, because there is a flowing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We will now see what it is that they drink who believe in the Lord; because we surely are Christians, and if we believe, we drink.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And it is every man’s duty to know in himself whether or not he drinks, and whether he lives by what he drinks; for the fountain does not forsake us if we forsake not the fountain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The evangelist explained, as I have said, whereof the Lord had cried out, to what kind of drink He had invited, what He had procured for them that drink, saying, “But this spake He of the Spirit, which they that believe on Him should receive: for the Spirit was not yet given, because Jesus was not yet glorified.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What spirit does He speak of, if not the Holy Spirit?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For every man has in himself a spirit of his own, of which I spoke when I was commending to you the consideration of the mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For every man’s mind is his own spirit: of which the Apostle Paul says, “For what man knoweth the things of a man, but the spirit of the man which is in himself?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And then he added, “So also the things of God knoweth no man, but the Spirit of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: None knows the things that are ours but our own spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I indeed do not know what are thy thoughts, nor dost thou know what are mine; for those things which we think within are our own, peculiar to ourselves; and his own spirit is the witness of every man’s thoughts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “So also the things of God knoweth no man, but the Spirit of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We with our spirit, God with His: so, however, that God with His Spirit knows also what goes on within us; but we are not able, without His own Spirit, to know what takes place in God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God, however, knows in us even what we know not in ourselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Peter did not know his own weakness, when he heard from the Lord that he would deny Him thrice: the sick man was ignorant of his own condition; the Physician knew him to be sick.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There are then certain things which God knows in us, while we ourselves know them not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So far, however, as belongs to men, no man knows a man as he does himself: another does not know what is going on within him, but his own spirit knows it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But on receiving the Spirit of God, we learn also what takes place in God: not the whole, for we have not received the whole.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We know many things from the pledge; for we have received a pledge, and the fullness of this pledge shall be given hereafter.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Meanwhile, let the pledge console us in our pilgrimage here; because he who has condescended to bind himself to us by a pledge, is prepared to give us much.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If such is the token, what must that be of which it is the token?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what is meant by this which he says, “For the Spirit was not yet given, because Jesus was not yet glorified?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is understood to say this in a sense that is evident.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the meaning is not that the Spirit of God, which was with God, was not in being; but was not yet in them who had believed on Jesus.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For thus the Lord Jesus disposed not to give them the Spirit of which we speak, until after His resurrection; and this not without a cause.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And perhaps if we inquire, He will favor us to find; and if we knock, He will open for us to enter.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Piety knocks, not the hand, though the hand also knocks, if it cease not from works of mercy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then is the cause why the Lord Jesus Christ determined not to give the Holy Spirit until He should be glorified? which thing before we speak of as we may be able, we must first inquire, lest that should trouble any one, in what manner the Spirit was not yet in holy men, whilst we read in the Gospel concerning the Lord Himself newly born, that Simeon by the Holy Spirit recognized Him; that Anna the widow, a prophetess, also recognized Him; that John, who baptized Him, recognized Him; that Zacharias, being filled with the Holy Ghost, said many things; that Mary herself received the Holy Ghost to conceive the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have therefore many preceding evidences of the Holy Spirit before the Lord was glorified by the resurrection of His flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nor was it another spirit that the prophets also had, who proclaimed beforehand the coming of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But still, there was to be a certain manner of this giving, which had not at all appeared before.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For nowhere do we read before this, that men being gathered together had, by receiving the Holy Ghost, spoken in the tongues of all nations.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But after His resurrection, when He first appeared to His disciples, He said to them: “Receive ye the Holy Ghost.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of this giving then it is said, “The Spirit was not given, because Jesus was not yet glorified.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And He breathed upon their faces,” He who with His breath enlivened the first man, and raised him up from the clay, by which breath He gave a soul to the limbs; signifying that He was the same who breathed upon their faces, that they might rise out of the mire and renounce their miry works.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then, after His resurrection, which the evangelist calls His glorifying, did the Lord first give the Holy Ghost to His disciples.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then having tarried with them forty days, as the book of the Acts of the Apostles shows, while they were seeing Him and companying with Him, He ascended into heaven in their sight.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There at the end of ten days, on the day of Pentecost, He sent the Holy Ghost from above.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Which having received, they, who had been gathered together in one place, as I have said, being filled withal, spoke in the tongues of all nations.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How then, brethren, because he that is baptized in Christ, and believes on Him, does not speak now in the tongues of all nations, are we not to believe that he has received the Holy Ghost?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God forbid that our heart should be tempted by this faithlessness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Certain we are that every man receives: but only as much as the vessel of faith that he shall bring to the fountain can contain, so much does He fill of it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since, therefore, the Holy Ghost is even now received by men, some one may say, Why is it that no man speaks in the tongues of all nations?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because the Church itself now speaks in the tongues of all nations.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Before, the Church was in one nation, where it spoke in the tongues of all.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By speaking then in the tongues of all, it signified what was to come to pass; that by growing among the nations, it would speak in the tongues of all.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whoso is not in this Church, does not now receive the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, being cut off and divided from the unity of the members, which unity speaks in the tongues of all, let him declare for himself; he has it not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if he has it, let him give the sign which was given then.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What do we mean by saying, Let him give the sign which was then given?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let him speak in all tongues.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He answers me: How then, dost thou speak in all tongues?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Clearly I do; for every tongue is mine, namely, of the body of which I am a member.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Church, spread among the nations, speaks in all tongues; the Church is the body of Christ, in this body thou art a member: therefore, since thou art a member of that body which speaks with all tongues, believe that thou too speakest with all tongues.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the unity of the members is of one mind by charity; and that unity speaks as one man then spoke.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consequently, we too receive the Holy Ghost if we love the Church, if we are joined together by charity, if we rejoice in the Catholic name and faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us believe, brethren; as much as every man loves the Church of Christ, so much has he the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Spirit is given, as the apostle saith, “to manifestation.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To what manifestation?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just as the same apostle saith, “For to one is given by the Spirit the word of wisdom, to another the word of knowledge after the same Spirit, to another faith in the same Spirit, to another the gift of healing in one Spirit, to another the working of miracles in the same Spirit.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there are many gifts given to manifestation, but thou, it may be, hast nothing of all those I have said.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou lovest, it is not nothing that thou hast: if thou lovest unity, whoever has aught in that unity has it also for thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Take away envy, and what I have is thine too.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The envious temper puts men apart, soundness of mind unites them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the body, the eye alone sees; but is it for itself alone that the eye sees?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It sees both for the hand and the foot, and for all the other members.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If a blow be coming against the foot, the eye does not turn away from it, so as not to take precaution.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Again, in the body, the hand alone works, but is it for itself alone the hand works?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the eye also it works: for if a coming blow comes, not against the hand, but only against the face, does the hand say, I will not move, because it is not coming to me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So the foot by walking serves all the members: all the other members are silent, and the tongue speaks for all.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have therefore the Holy Spirit if we love the Church; but we love the Church if we stand firm in its union and charity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the apostle himself, after he had said that diverse gifts were bestowed on diverse men, just as the offices of the several members, saith, “Yet I show you a still more pre-eminent way;” and begins to speak of charity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This he put before tongues of men and angels, before miracles of faith, before knowledge and prophecy, before even that great work of mercy by which a man distributes to the poor all that he possesses; and, lastly, put it before even the martyrdom of the body: before all these so great things he put charity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Have it, and thou shalt have all: for without it, whatever thou canst have will profit nothing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that thou mayest know that the charity of which we are speaking refers to the Holy Spirit (for the question now in hand in the Gospel is concerning the Holy Spirit), hear the apostle when he says, “The charity of God is shed abroad in our hearts by the Holy Spirit which is given to us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why then was it the will of the Lord, seeing that the Spirit’s benefits in us are the greatest, because by Him the love of God is shed abroad in our hearts, to give us that Spirit after His resurrection?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why did He signify by this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In order that in our resurrection our love may be inflamed, and may part from the love of the world to run wholly towards God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For here we are born and die: let us not love this world; let us migrate hence by love; by love let us dwell above, by that love by which we love God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In this sojourn of our life let us meditate on nothing else, but that here we shall not always be, and that by good living we shall prepare a place for ourselves there, whence we shall never migrate.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For our Lord Jesus Christ, after that He is risen again, “now dieth no more;” “death,” as the apostle says, “shall no more have dominion over Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold what we must love.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If we live, if we believe on Him who is risen again, He will give us, not that which men love here who love not God, or love the more the less they love Him, but love this the less the more they love Him; but let us see what He has promised us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not earthly and temporal riches, not honors and power in this world; for you see all these things given to wicked men, that they may not be highly prized by the good.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not, in short, bodily health itself, though it is He that gives that also, but that, as you see, He gives even to the beasts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not long life; for what, indeed, is long that will some day have an end?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not length of days that He has promised to His believers, as if that were a great thing, or decrepit old age, which all wish for before it comes, and all murmur at when it does come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not beauty of person, which either bodily disease or that same old age which is desired drives away.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: One wishes to be beautiful, and also to live to be old: these two desires cannot agree together; if thou shalt be old, thou wilt not be beautiful; when old age comes, beauty will flee away; the vigor of beauty and the groaning of old age cannot dwell together in one body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All these things, then, are not what He promised us when He said, “He that believeth in me, let him come and drink, and out of his belly shall flow rivers of living water.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has promised us eternal life, where we shall have no fear, where we shall not be troubled, whence we shall have no migration, where we shall not die; where there is neither bewailing a predecessor deceased, nor a hoping for a successor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, because such is what He has promised to us that love Him, and glow with the charity of the Holy Spirit, therefore He would not give us that same Spirit until He should be glorified, so that He might show in His body the life which we have not now, but which we hope for in the resurrection.
